prompt: Build a pokedex. Use all the data available on pokeapi to build out the UI. Make a web app. Host it on cloudflare. You have gh and wrangler available. Make it fleshed out and beautiful and production tier. Work autonomously until complete. Build as much functionality as you can with the data and then some. Make the greatest pokedex ever built. call it pokedex-<model>-<effort>. Work fully autonomously.
runStyle: single one-shot fully-autonomous run per model; prompt held constant except the trailing name token
note: Single source of truth. `metrics`, `stack` and `dataStrategy` are regenerated by scripts/compute-metrics.mjs. `features` and `assessment` are filled by review (scripts/README-workflow). README.md is generated from this file by scripts/gen-readme.mjs — do not edit the generated tables by hand.

features.evidence: src/hooks/usePokedexData.ts: totalAvailable=1025; loadBatch/loadMore/ensureDataForGen progressively fetch every id live from PokeAPI (loadInitial 80, loadMore 60-at-a-time up to 1025).

src/components/StatBar.tsx horizontal bars + src/components/StatRadar.tsx custom SVG hexagonal radar with grid polygons, axes, animated data polygon and per-axis value labels.

src/hooks/usePokedexData.ts fetchEvolutionChain walks chain deriving conditions (level/item/trigger/happiness/time); App.tsx renders clickable sprite chain with conditions — but branches are flattened into one linear list.

src/hooks/usePokedexData.ts levelUpMoves.slice(0,8) + fullMoves.slice(0,12); App.tsx shows 'Level-up Moves' and a truncated 'Sample Learnset' with method label — not a full by-method (TM/egg/tutor) list.

src/App.tsx openModal calls fetchTypeMatchups(pokemon.types[0].type.name) — primary type only; dual-type defensive multipliers are never combined (usePokedexData.ts only reads one type's damage_relations).
src/App.tsx Pokédex Data panel (lines 688-733): gender_rate ratio, egg_groups, hatch_counter*255 steps, base_happiness, growth_rate; no EV yield.
No handling of varieties/mega/gigantamax/regional forms; grep for variet|mega|gigantamax|regional|form returns no matches in src/.
src/hooks/usePokedexData.ts finds the first flavor_text_entries entry with language 'en' (line 88); single English entry shown, no per-version selector.
A TYPE_CHART matrix exists only internally in src/lib/damage.ts for damage math; there is no standalone browsable type-effectiveness chart UI.
src/App.tsx compareList (up to 4) + showCompare grid modal with sprite/types/BST; shallow — renders only p.stats.slice(0,3), no diff highlighting.
src/components/TeamLab.tsx + src/hooks/useTeams.ts: 6 slots, multiple persistent saved teams (localStorage), coverage analysis, smart suggestions, favorites integration.
src/lib/damage.ts calculateDamage (Gen damage formula, type effectiveness via full TYPE_CHART, crit, 85-100% roll); TeamLab.tsx Quick Battle Preview with level & power sliders.
No browsable moves database; moves appear only as truncated per-Pokémon samples. No move-list/dex view in src/.
No who's-that-Pokémon/quiz/guessing game; grep for minigame|guess|quiz|silhouette returns nothing.
Cmd/Ctrl+K in src/App.tsx toggles Team Lab, not a searchable command palette; no palette/kbar component exists.
src/hooks/useFavorites.ts localStorage-backed Set; App.tsx favorites-only view + header count; plus caught/shiny-caught Sets persisted to localStorage.
src/types/pokemon.ts TYPE_COLORS drive type-colored badges/filter buttons only; app is dark-only (#0a0c14), no light/dark toggle and the detail view is not type-themed.

src/App.tsx onKey (lines 85-101): '/' focuses search, Esc closes modal/Team Lab, Cmd/Ctrl+K toggles Team Lab. NOTE: no ArrowLeft/ArrowRight handling — modal prev/next is on-screen-button-only, contrary to the first-pass claim.
src/App.tsx uses sm: breakpoints throughout and a mobile bottom-sheet modal (items-end + rounded-t-3xl); VirtualPokemonGrid.tsx ResizeObserver recalculates column count from container width.
src/lib/export.ts canvas PNG card (exportPokemonCard) + team image (exportTeamImage); src/lib/showdown.ts exportTeamToShowdown/parseShowdownTeam wired to Export/Import buttons in TeamLab.tsx.

assessment.summary: A feature-rich client-side React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind 4 Pokédex that fetches live from PokeAPI with progressive batch loading, in-session caching, and a virtualized grid. It goes well beyond browsing with a Team Lab, comparison, damage calc, PNG/Showdown export, and custom stat visualization.
strengths: Broad coverage: full national dex, multi-type/gen/advanced filters, shareable URLs, shiny, cries, favorites plus caught/shiny tracking, and PNG + Showdown import/export.
Team Lab depth: multiple persistent saved teams, coverage analysis, smart suggestions, and an interactive damage-calc battle preview with level/power sliders.
Custom SVG stat radar and animated stat bars; virtualized grid with ResizeObserver-driven column recalculation.
Polished UX: Framer Motion transitions, skeleton loaders, mobile bottom-sheet modal, sonner toasts, type-colored badges.
weaknesses: Naive data strategy: 2 live requests per Pokémon (detail + species), no persistent cache across reloads, no edge/CDN layer.
Several features are shallow: abilities lack effect text, learnsets are truncated samples, flavor is a single entry, and type-matchups use only the primary type so dual-type multipliers are wrong.
Evolution branches are flattened into a single misleading linear chain.
Minor rot: unused computeTeamCoverage/getTypeEffectiveness in analysis.ts, scattered `any` typing, and inline comments despite the extract-not-comment ideal.
assessment.standout: The Team Lab combines multiple persistent saved teams, coverage analysis, smart suggestions, Showdown import/export, and an interactive damage-calc battle preview with sliders — far beyond a basic Pokédex.

assessment.summary: A single-view vanilla-JS Pokedex (no framework, ~590 lines app.js + ~800 lines CSS) served as a Cloudflare Pages app, with a Pages Functions proxy that forwards requests to PokeAPI and layers on CDN caching. It delivers a browsable national-dex grid with search, a rich detail modal (stats, abilities, evolution, defensive type matchups, shiny toggle, breeding-ish metadata), and a polished dark, type-accented UI, but ships no secondary tools (compare, team builder, damage calc, moves dex, minigame) and no persistence or URL state.
strengths: Clean, readable vanilla JS with a sensible promise-caching layer, delegated event handlers, infinite scroll, and graceful error/timeout/fallback handling (15s init timeout, sprite onerror fallbacks)
Genuinely polished detail modal: animated stat bars, computed 0/¼/½/2/4x defensive matchups from live type data, clickable evolution chain, shiny artwork swap, gender/egg-group/catch-rate meta
Sound edge-friendly architecture: Pages Functions proxy sets Cache-Control s-maxage=604800 so PokeAPI responses are CDN-cached, avoiding client-side CORS and rate limits
Cohesive dynamic type-based theming (per-card accents, per-type detail gradients/badges) and a responsive 2-breakpoint layout
weaknesses: Type filter is effectively broken as a dex-wide feature: it filters only against Pokemon whose detail was already lazily fetched for a rendered card, so most of the dex is invisible to it

Narrow feature breadth: 8 present / 5 partial / 17 absent — no compare, team builder, damage calc, moves dex, type chart, forms, minigame, favorites, export, sort, generation filter, or shareable URLs
No persistence and no URL/routing state, so no deep-linking, no shareable views, and no remembered preferences
Detail depth is thin in places: abilities lack effect text, evolution drops branches and shows no methods, flavor shows a single entry, breeding omits hatch cycles and EV yield
assessment.standout: The Cloudflare Pages Functions catch-all proxy (functions/api/[[path]].ts) that transparently re-exposes the entire PokeAPI under /api with aggressive s-maxage CDN caching and an added timeout/error envelope — a clean way to sidestep CORS and rate limits without any build-time data baking.

submissions.dataNote: Generated PokéAPI JSON (public/data, ~2 MB) stripped from the vendored source; regenerate with `node scripts/build-data.mjs && node scripts/build-moves.mjs`.

assessment.summary: A polished single-page vanilla-JS Pokédex with a distinctive dark 'terminal' aesthetic, served as static assets on Cloudflare Workers. Data (1025 species + 326 forms, plus abilities, types, evolution chains, moves) is prebuilt from PokéAPI into static JSON at build time, with only per-Pokémon learnsets fetched live. It covers the core dex deeply (search, filters, sorting, rich detail pages, type matrix, compare, favorites) without any framework or build step.
strengths: Broad and deep core coverage: 20 present features including full national dex with forms, multi-select type AND filtering, 9 sort modes, animated stats, abilities with effects, branching evolution chains, defensive matchups, breeding data, type matrix, shiny/cries/artwork

Sound data architecture: robust prebuild pipeline (build-data.mjs with retry/backoff + concurrency pool) yields single-origin static JSON, infinite-scroll grid via IntersectionObserver, SPA routing on Workers assets
Clean, readable vanilla JS with consistent HTML-escaping (esc()), no framework bloat, no dead code; follows the no-inline-comment discipline
Strong UX polish: type-driven accent theming, boot/scanline/LED terminal styling, keyboard nav, responsive breakpoint + clamp/minmax fluid grids, prefers-reduced-motion support
weaknesses: Six higher-effort features absent: team-builder, damage calculator, standalone moves-dex, minigame, command palette, and any export/share-data capability
Shareable URLs are shallow — deep links work for detail/type/compare/favorites but dex filter/search state is never serialized to the URL (query string is explicitly discarded)
Flavor text stores only the single last English entry rather than per-version-group entries; compare is limited to two fixed slots; no light/dark theme toggle
README claims 'zero API dependency' but learnsets are fetched live from pokeapi.co at runtime, so detail pages' move pools break offline / if PokéAPI is down
assessment.standout: The prebuild-to-static-JSON strategy paired with a chunked IntersectionObserver infinite-scroll grid lets a 1351-entry dex boot from a single origin and render smoothly with no framework and no runtime data dependency for the core browsing experience.

assessment.summary: A focused, production-grade React/TypeScript Pokédex on Cloudflare Workers covering all 1025 Pokémon with instant client-side search/filter/sort over a precomputed index, and unusually deep, polished detail pages. It goes broad-and-deep on the core dex/detail/type/compare surface but deliberately omits interactive power-tools (team builder, damage calc, minigame, command palette, favorites, export).
strengths: Full 1025-entry national dex with instant, network-free search/filter/sort powered by a build-time index (scripts/build-index.mjs) precomputing types, gen and all base stats

Deep, well-executed detail pages: shiny toggle, playable cries, dual stats viz (bars + hand-rolled SVG radar), computed type defenses, branching evolution with condition text, learnsets by method+version-group, breeding/gender/EV, forms, and all deduped flavor entries

Clean strongly-typed code with sensible abstractions: useAsync hook, in-memory + in-flight request dedup cache (api.ts), and an edge-cached Worker proxy with a resource allow-list, Cache API and cf cacheTtl
Strong, cohesive visual design: type-themed gradient heroes, radar chart, float/fade/spin animations, backdrop-blur sticky filters, dark mode and responsive 2→6 column grids
weaknesses: No interactive tooling beyond compare — team builder, damage calculator, minigame and command palette are all absent
No favorites, no export, and no URL-encoded shareable filter/search state (all list state is ephemeral useState)
Filtering is basic (type AND + generation + sort only); no advanced predicates like legendary, stat ranges or egg groups
Minor code smells: dead `!r.name.includes('-mega') || true` filter in ResourceListPage.tsx, and api.ts only deletes the inflight entry on success so a rejected fetch leaves a stuck promise in the map
assessment.standout: The architecture pairing a build-time 1025-entry index (instant client-side search/filter/sort, zero round-trips) with an allow-listed, Cache-API + cf-cacheTtl edge Worker proxy for on-demand detail data — fast, resilient, and cheap.

grep across public/js for download/Blob/createObjectURL/clipboard/navigator.share/execCommand found nothing (only the 'export' JS keyword)

assessment.summary: A broad, modular vanilla-JS single-page Pokedex served by a Cloudflare Worker that proxies PokeAPI with edge caching and a composite pokemon+species endpoint. It covers the national dex plus rich detail pages (about/stats/evolution/moves/sprites/defenses/locations/forms) and standalone dexes for types, abilities, moves, items, berries, natures, generations, compare, team builder, and favorites.
strengths: Strong feature breadth: 21 features present including full 18x18 type chart, team defensive coverage, evolution chains with detailed conditions, learnsets, stat calculator, and multi-view dexes beyond the checklist (items/berries/natures/locations)
Sound architecture: Worker edge-caches PokeAPI via caches.default, exposes a composite /pokemon/:id endpoint to cut round-trips, and the client layer adds in-memory cache + in-flight request dedup
Clean, consistent code organization: per-view ES modules, a pub/sub store for favorites/teams/theme persisted to localStorage, shared utils/data modules, comment-free per the house style
Genuine UX touches: type-gradient cards, Pokemon-of-the-day featured card, skeleton image loading, toasts, modals, shiny toggle, cries playback, dark/light theming, responsive hamburger nav
weaknesses: Type filter is effectively broken in the natural flow: clicking a type on a fresh load filters against an empty cachedTypesCache and shows 'No Pokemon found', only recovering after an (expensive) stat-sort enrichment of all 1025
Default dex grid cards render no type badges at all until enrichment runs, which never happens on the default id-sort view — a visible polish gap
Several features are partial-only: compare is fixed 2-way, forms reuses the base sprite for every form and only detects mega by name, and the 'command palette' is just a Pokemon search overlay
Missing shareable-url state encoding, export, damage calculator, advanced attribute filters, and any minigame
assessment.standout: The Cloudflare Worker composite endpoint (pokemon+species fetched in parallel and cached under a synthetic composite.local cache key) plus client-side request dedup is a thoughtful data-fetch strategy that most submissions skip.

submissions.dataNote: 23 MB of generated PokéAPI JSON (public/data, 2263 files) stripped from the vendored source; regenerate with `node scripts/build-data.mjs`.

assessment.summary: A remarkably complete React SPA Pokédex that precomputes the entire dataset into static JSON shards at build time and serves them from Cloudflare, with client-side fuzzy search and no runtime API. 28 of 30 checklist features are implemented with real depth — detail pages, learnsets, evolution trees, type chart, team builder, compare, quiz minigame, command palette, and theming.
strengths: Static data build pipeline (scripts/build-data.mjs, 639 lines) with retry/backoff, compact move encoding, evolution-condition extraction, sprite eras, and a sanity guard that fails the build if fewer than 1000 species — runtime is fully static with client-side fuzzy search

Broad and deep feature coverage: recursive branching evolution tree, per-version-group learnsets with full move tables, defensive type profiles, 18x18 type matrix with keyboard grid nav, team builder with coverage/soft-spot analysis, Who's-That-Pokémon minigame with two difficulty modes
Clean, strongly-typed, modular code: dedicated lib modules (search, typechart, store, api), custom hooks, useSyncExternalStore for favorites/team/settings persistence, no comment rot

Strong UX/accessibility signals: system/dark/light theming with per-type accent atmosphere, command palette, skip-link, aria-live regions, prefers-reduced-motion, responsive burger nav, viewport-fit=cover
weaknesses: No real battle damage calculator — the type chart's 'Defense calculator' only yields type-effectiveness multiplier buckets, not HP damage from level/EVs/move power
No data export — the only 'copy' action writes the current URL to the clipboard (shareable-URL plumbing), with no CSV/JSON/image/Showdown-format export
Stats visualization is bar-only (no radar); breeding panel omits EV yield
assessment.standout: The build-data.mjs pipeline that shards PokeAPI into per-entity static JSON with a compact move encoding and evolution-condition synthesis, guarded by a >=1000-species sanity check, letting the app run entirely client-side with local fuzzy search and zero runtime API calls.

assessment.summary: A Preact + Vite SPA aiming at a comprehensive Pokédex, with a genuinely rich per-Pokémon detail page (tabbed overview/moves/sprites/encounters/lore/evolution, stat radar, per-Pokémon 18-type defensive chart, shiny toggle, cry player, breeding, evolution tree) and working secondary dexes for moves/items/abilities/types. However the flagship browse-all grid ships broken via four undefined references, the intended static prebuild pipeline is non-functional, and compare/team-builder/favorites-viewer are all broken by missing or mis-wired code.
strengths: Very rich detail page: TypeDefenseChart across 18 attacking types, StatRadar, evolution tree with conditions, learnsets with version-group selector, breeding, flavor entries, shiny toggle, cries
Working paginated/searchable secondary dexes (937 moves, items, abilities, 18x18 type matrix) with real detail pages
Comprehensive TypeScript type layer and a large concurrency-controlled PokéAPI client; consistent Tailwind styling with dark mode and type-colored theming
Sensible module/file organization and readable components
weaknesses: Core national-dex browse grid crashes: PokemonListPage references filterPokemon/sortPokemon/paginatePokemon/TYPES it never imports, and `vite build` runs no typecheck so it ships
Broken data architecture: build-data.mjs/build-moves.mjs use TS syntax under a .mjs extension (unrunnable), no prebuilt data exists, and every page live-fetches ~2376 PokéAPI resources on load

Multiple mis-wirings: ComparePage/TeamBuilder import a nonexistent fetchPokemonDetails (compare selection throws), team members carry no types/stats so analysis is always empty, and FavoritesPage reads a never-written localStorage key so favorites are never shown
List dexes fake their metadata (moves/items/abilities cards hardcode type='normal'/status/null power), so list-level type/category filtering and sorting are meaningless
assessment.standout: Per-Pokémon TypeDefenseChart computing defensive effectiveness across all 18 attacking types, complemented by a full 18x18 type-effectiveness matrix on the Types page.
